articles.title: Hackers exploit Marimo flaw to deploy NKAbuse malware from Hugging Face
articles.summary: Hackers are exploiting a critical vulnerability in Marimo reactive Python notebook to deploy a new variant of NKAbuse malware hosted on Hugging Face Spaces. [...]

articles.title: Google expands Gemini AI use to fight malicious ads on its platform
articles.summary: Google says it is increasingly using its Gemini AI models to detect and block harmful ads on its advertising platforms, as scammers and threat actors continue to evolve their tactics to evade detection. [...]

articles.title: New ATHR vishing platform uses AI voice agents for automated attacks
articles.summary: A new cybercrime platform called ATHR can harvest credentials via fully automated voice phishing attacks that use both human operators and AI agents for the social engineering phase. [...]

articles.title: Cargo thieving hackers running sophisticated remote access campaigns, researchers find
articles.summary: Losses from cargo theft in North America rose to $6.6 billion in 2025, driven largely by digital attacks, according to the fleet management company Geotab.

articles.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Defender 0-Day, SonicWall Brute-Force, 17-Year-Old Excel RCE and 15 More Stories
articles.summary: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? Yeah. This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you ignore the whole "crime" part, ancient vulnerabilities somehow still ruining people's day

articles.title: Data Breach at Tennessee Hospital Affects 337,000
articles.summary: Cookeville Regional Medical Center was targeted last year by the Rhysida ransomware group, which stole 500GB of data. The post Data Breach at Tennessee Hospital Affects 337,000 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Artemis Emerges From Stealth With $70 Million in Funding
articles.summary: The startup is leveraging AI to prevent AI-powered attacks across applications, users, machines, and cloud workloads. The post Artemis Emerges From Stealth With $70 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Ukrainian emergency services and hospitals hit by espionage campaign using new AgingFly malware
articles.summary: Hackers have targeted Ukrainian hospitals and local government bodies in a new espionage campaign using a malware tool dubbed AgingFly, researchers say.

The following versions of AVEVA Pipeline Simulation are affected: Pipeline Simulation <=2025_SP1_build_7.1.9497.635

articles.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
articles.summary: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-34197 Apache ActiveMQ Improper Input Validation Vulnerability This type of vulnerability is a frequent attack vector for malicious cyber actors and poses sign

articles.title: Splunk Enterprise Update Patches Code Execution Vulnerability
articles.summary: The flaw allows low-privileged users to upload files to a temporary directory to achieve remote code execution. The post Splunk Enterprise Update Patches Code Execution Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
articles.summary: Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate any user within the service. The details of the vulnerabilities are below - CVE-2026-20184 (

articles.title: Microsoft Paid Out $2.3 Million at Zero Day Quest 2026 Hacking Contest
articles.summary: Researchers found more than 80 high-impact cloud and AI vulnerabilities during the event, which had a $5 million prize pool. The post Microsoft Paid Out $2.3 Million at Zero Day Quest 2026 Hacking Contest appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: NIST Prioritizes NVD Enrichment for CVEs in CISA KEV, Critical Software
articles.summary: To optimize management of CVE volume, entries that do not meet specific criteria will not be automatically enriched. The post NIST Prioritizes NVD Enrichment for CVEs in CISA KEV, Critical Software appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Data breach at edtech giant McGraw Hill affects 13.5 million accounts
articles.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion group has leaked data from 13.5 million McGraw Hill user accounts, stolen after breaching the company's Salesforce environment earlier this month. [...]

articles.title: Obsidian Plugin Abuse Delivers PHANTOMPULSE RAT in Targeted Finance, Crypto Attacks
articles.summary: A "novel" social engineering campaign has been observed abusing Obsidian, a cross-platform note-taking application, as an initial access vector to distribute a previously undocumented Windows remote access trojan called PHANTOMPULSE in attacks targeting individuals in the financial and cryptocurrenc

articles.title: Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerabilities in Webex, ISE
articles.summary: The flaws can be exploited remotely to impersonate users or execute arbitrary commands on the underlying OS. The post Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerabilities in Webex, ISE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Ransomware Hits Automotive Data Expert Autovista
articles.summary: The automotive analysis and data company is working with external experts to investigate the attack. The post Ransomware Hits Automotive Data Expert Autovista appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot Agents Vulnerable to Prompt Injection via Comments
articles.summary: A researcher has disclosed the details of the AI attack method he has named ‘Comment and Control’. The post Claude Code, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot Agents Vulnerable to Prompt Injection via Comments appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: UAC-0247 Targets Ukrainian Clinics and Government in Data-Theft Malware Campaign
articles.summary: The Computer Emergencies Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has disclosed details of a new campaign that has targeted governments and municipal healthcare institutions, mainly clinics and emergency hospitals, to deliver malware capable of stealing sensitive data from Chromium-based we

articles.title: Critical Nginx UI auth bypass flaw now actively exploited in the wild
articles.summary: A critical vulnerability in Nginx UI with Model Context Protocol (MCP) support is now being exploited in the wild for full server takeover without authentication. [...]

articles.title: New AgingFly malware used in attacks on Ukraine govt, hospitals
articles.summary: A new malware family named 'AgingFly' has been identified in attacks against local governments and hospitals that steal authentication data from Chromium-based browsers and WhatsApp messenger. [...]

articles.title: WordPress plugin suite hacked to push malware to thousands of sites
articles.summary: More than 30 WordPress plugins in the EssentialPlugin package have been compromised with malicious code that allows unauthorized access to websites running them. [...]

articles.title: NIST to limit work on CVE entries as submissions surge
articles.summary: NIST said it will only add details and information to the records of vulnerabilities that meet a certain threshold — changing a longstanding mission to categorize every CVE, which stands for cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures.

articles.title: Teen arrested in Northern Ireland over cyberattack on school network
articles.summary: A 16-year-old boy has been arrested in Northern Ireland after a cyberattack disrupted access to educational systems used by potentially hundreds of thousands of students.

articles.title: n8n Webhooks Abused Since October 2025 to Deliver Malware via Phishing Emails
articles.summary: Threat actors have been observed weaponizing n8n, a popular artificial intelligence (AI) workflow automation platform, to facilitate sophisticated phishing campaigns and deliver malicious payloads or fingerprint devices by sending automated emails. "By leveraging trusted infrastructur

articles.title: Sweden Blames Pro-Russian Group for Cyberattack Last Year on Its Energy Infrastructure
articles.summary: In what was Sweden’s first public mention of the attack, the country’s minister for civil defense said it targeted a heating plant in western Sweden. The post Sweden Blames Pro-Russian Group for Cyberattack Last Year on Its Energy Infrastructure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: UK warns businesses to address cyber risks amid Anthropic AI panic
articles.summary: The British government warned businesses to strengthen their cyber defenses amid concerns prompted by the release of Anthropic's Mythos over how artificial intelligence could reshape the threat landscape.

articles.title: CISA flags Windows Task Host vulnerability as exploited in attacks
articles.summary: CISA warned U.S. government agencies to secure their systems against a Windows Task Host privilege escalation vulnerability that could allow attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges. [...]

articles.title: Exploited Vulnerability Exposes Nginx Servers to Hacking
articles.summary: Hackers are exploiting CVE-2026-33032, a critical remote takeover vulnerability in the Nginx UI management tool. The post Exploited Vulnerability Exposes Nginx Servers to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Educational company McGraw Hill says Salesforce misconfiguration led to data leak
articles.summary: The data breach emerged this weekend when the ShinyHunters cybercriminal organization claimed to have stolen 45 million Salesforce records and threatened to leak the information by April 14 if a ransom was not paid.

articles.title: Rolling Networks: Securing the Transportation Sector
articles.summary: Modern trucks are rolling networks packed with sensors, connectivity, and attack surfaces, creating new cyber risks. NMFTA's Cybersecurity Conference brings industry leaders together to tackle emerging threats in transportation. [...]

articles.title: ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks
articles.summary: Researchers warn that a flaw in Anthropic’s Model Context Protocol allows unsanitized commands to execute silently, enabling full system compromise across widely used AI environments. The post ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: 100 Chrome Extensions Steal User Data, Create Backdoor
articles.summary: Published through five accounts, the extensions appear part of a coordinated campaign based on shared C&C infrastructure. The post 100 Chrome Extensions Steal User Data, Create Backdoor appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISO Conversations: Ross McKerchar, CISO at Sophos
articles.summary: Sophos’ Ross McKerchar discusses leadership at scale, retaining talent, defending against AI-enabled threats, and the industry’s growing trust problem. The post CISO Conversations: Ross McKerchar, CISO at Sophos appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting nginx-ui, an open-source, web-based Nginx management tool, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-33032 (CVSS score: 9.8), an authentication bypass vulnerability that enables threat actors t

articles.title: April Patch Tuesday Fixes Critical Flaws Across SAP, Adobe, Microsoft, Fortinet, and More
articles.summary: A number of critical vulnerabilities impacting products from Adobe, Fortinet, Microsoft, and SAP have taken center stage in April's Patch Tuesday releases. Topping the list is an SQL injection vulnerability impacting SAP Business Planning and Consolidation and SAP Business Warehouse (

articles.title: Sweden says pro-Russian hackers attempted to breach thermal power plant
articles.summary: A suspected pro-Russian hacker group attempted to disrupt operations at a thermal power plant in western Sweden last year, a Swedish defense official said.

articles.title: Two Vulnerabilities Patched in Ivanti Neurons for ITSM
articles.summary: The flaws could allow a remote attacker to maintain access after their account has been disabled and to access information from other user sessions. The post Two Vulnerabilities Patched in Ivanti Neurons for ITSM appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: $10 Domain Could Have Handed Hackers 25k Endpoints, Including in OT and Gov Networks
articles.summary: Researchers found adware capable of killing cybersecurity products and pushing more dangerous payloads to infected systems. The post $10 Domain Could Have Handed Hackers 25k Endpoints, Including in OT and Gov Networks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Fortinet Patches Critical FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The flaws could allow attackers to bypass authentication or execute arbitrary code or commands via HTTP requests. The post Fortinet Patches Critical FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft Issues Patches for SharePoint Zero-Day and 168 Other New Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Microsoft on Tuesday released updates to address a record 169 security flaws across its product portfolio, including one vulnerability that has been actively exploited in the wild. Of these 169 vulnerabilities, 157 are rated Important, eight are rated Critical, three ar

articles.title: Scanning for AI Models, (Tue, Apr 14th)
articles.summary: Starting March 10, 2026, my DShield sensor started getting probe for various AI models such as claude, openclaw, huggingface, etc. Reviewing the data already reported by other DShield sensors to ISC, the DShield database shows reporting of these probes started that day and has been active ever since

articles.title: Microsoft adds Windows protections for malicious Remote Desktop files
articles.summary: Microsoft has introduced new Windows protections to defend against phishing attacks that abuse Remote Desktop connection (.rdp) files, adding warnings and disabling risky shared resources by default. [...]

articles.title: Crypto-exchange Kraken extorted by hackers after insider breach
articles.summary: The Kraken cryptocurrency exchange announced that a cybercrime group is trying to extort the company by threatening to release videos showing internal systems that host client data. [...]

articles.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
articles.summary: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth

articles.title: New ‘JanaWare’ ransomware targeting Turkish citizens as cybercriminal ecosystem fragments
articles.summary: The researchers said the ransomware operation has been ongoing since 2020 and is associated with a strain of malware that enforces execution constraints based on system locale and external IP geolocation.

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Exploited SharePoint Zero-Day and 160 Other Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Experts say this is the second-largest Microsoft Patch Tuesday ever based on CVE count. The post Microsoft Patches Exploited SharePoint Zero-Day and 160 Other Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.
